tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2
tool_args.raw_text: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 will extend from the northern limit of SAS Phase 1 at 105 St, proceed north under Second Ave, and then turn west along 125 St. Three new stations at 106 St, 116 St, and 125 St. Budget $6.968B. Substantial Completion Q3 2032.

payload.description: Extend Q line from 105 St north under Second Ave then west along 125 St, with three new stations at 106 St, 116 St, and 125 St. Budget $6.968B. On time and on budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 (SAS2) will extend from the northern limit of SAS Phase 1 at 105 St, proceed north under Second Ave, and then turn west along 125 St
Original Forecast Substantial Completion Q3 2032 Q3 2032 Budget $6.968B $6.968B
Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 is currently on time and on budget

tool_args.label: Interborough Express
tool_args.raw_text: Interborough Express (IBX) will provide a transformative new transit connection between Brooklyn and Queens along an existing rail corridor from Sunset Park to Jackson Heights. Light rail transit system with 19 stations over 14 miles, 32-minute end-to-end runtime.

payload.description: Light rail transit connecting Brooklyn and Queens from Sunset Park to Jackson Heights, 19 stations, 14-mile corridor, approximately 32 minute end-to-end runtime, projected daily ridership of 160,000.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Interborough Express (IBX) will provide a transformative new transit connection between Brooklyn and Queens. IBX will be built along an existing rail corridor running from Sunset Park to Jackson Heights
project's runtime from end to end is now projected at just 32 minutes, leading to a revised daily ridership projection of 160,000

tool_args.raw_text: With 780 feet of track now installed and more than half of the total bridge replacements completed, Phase One to the south and Phase Two to the north are proceeding simultaneously and efficiently, yielding cost savings.

evidence_refs.source_quote: With 780 feet of track now installed and more than half of the total bridge replacements completed, Phase One to the south and Phase Two to the north are proceeding simultaneously and efficiently, yielding cost savings.

tool_args.label: MTA Transitioning to 5G DCS for CBTC
tool_args.raw_text: 5G-based DCS is a dramatic technological improvement over the wi-fi based DCS system that the MTA currently uses for radio communication between the carbone CBTC controllers and the wayside CBTC zone controllers.

payload.claim_text: MTA intends to use 5G-based DCS on its new CBTC installations. 5G provides enhanced transmission capacity, enables cybersecurity features, and provides access to a competitive global radio equipment market.
payload.subject: MTA CBTC program

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA intends to use 5G-based DCS on its new CBTC installations
MTA opted to adopt a new 5G communications system with cybersecurity protection and advanced diagnostics

tool_args.label: CBTC-Only Systems Without Legacy Redundancy
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA is now ready to move forward with CBTC-only systems. The G line project is the first where only CBTC is being installed.

payload.claim_text: Until recently, each CBTC project included both a new CBTC system and a legacy mechanical system as a redundancy. The MTA is now ready to move forward with CBTC-only systems. The G line project is the first where only CBTC is being installed.
payload.subject: MTA CBTC program

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA is now ready to move forward with CBTC-only systems. The G line project is the first where only CBTC is being installed
